overview.summary: A sophisticated cyber espionage campaign named 'Operation Sharpshooter' targeting the communications sector aims to exfiltrate sensitive data while maintaining stealth and persistence. The attackers employ a multi-stage approach involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, Compromised Account, Public IP/Network Block, File, Logon Session, Target System Artifact
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email, Network Traffic Flow, Response Metadata, Delivery Method, Bash Script, Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder, Application Log Content, File Access, Network Service Scanning, Network Traffic Flow, Logon Session Creation, Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, File Modification

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers engage in active scanning activities to identify potential targets within the communications sector by mapping network nodes, hosts, devices, and routes.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: Operation Sharpshooter sets up phishing domains mimicking legitimate communication services to support their operations.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Attackers send crafted emails to employees pretending to be from trusted vendors or colleagues, leading to initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Upon successful phishing, attackers execute malware using command-line interfaces via bash scripts.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.technique_name: T1060: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
stage.capec: CAPEC-270: Modification of Registry Run Keys
stage.description: To maintain persistence, attackers add entries to the registry ensuring malware runs every time the system boots up.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Attackers gain higher-level permissions on compromised systems by exploiting public-facing applications.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Attackers avoid detection by disabling antivirus software to evade defenses.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: Stolen credentials allow attackers to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Attackers map out the internal network structure to understand the environment.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, attackers move through the network to reach critical systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Attackers gather sensitive data relevant to their espionage goals.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Collected data is transferred to attacker-controlled servers over a command and control channel.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: As a demonstration of capability and to cause disruption, attackers destroy data within the network.
